title: International Student Mobility Dashboard
subtitle: Global Higher Education Flows & COVID-19 Impact, 2015-2023
description: Interactive visualization of global student flows across higher education institutions. Enables exploration of which countries students come from, where they go, and how the COVID-19 pandemic disrupted and reshaped academic mobility. Features directional flow mapping, year-over-year trends, and deep-dive analysis of major mobility corridors.
keyInsight: Global student mobility dropped 30-40% in 2020-2021 during pandemic restrictions, with partial recovery by 2023. USA, UK, and Australia remain dominant destinations, with China and India as primary sources, but emerging diversification to alternate destinations like Canada and Singapore.

dataSource.name: Resilient but Uneven: International Student Mobility Reconfiguration Through COVID-19
dataSource.description: Research dataset tracking bilateral international student flows from origin to destination countries, 2015-2023, with focus on pandemic impact and recovery

analyticalApproach: Temporal trend analysis (pre-COVID baseline vs. pandemic vs. recovery)
Comparative impact assessment (2019 pre-COVID vs. 2023 post-COVID)

dashboardFeatures.name: Interactive Flow Map
dashboardFeatures.description: Visualization of student flows between countries with arrow sizing proportional to flow volume

dashboardFeatures.name: Directional Toggle
dashboardFeatures.description: Switch between 'Incoming' (which countries send students to destination) and 'Outgoing' (where students from origin go)

dashboardFeatures.name: Country Selector
dashboardFeatures.description: Focus on specific country or view global aggregate flows

dashboardFeatures.name: Year Slider
dashboardFeatures.description: Explore flows across 2015-2023 to track COVID-19 impact and recovery

dashboardFeatures.name: Top Sources Chart
dashboardFeatures.description: Horizontal bar chart showing which countries send most international students

dashboardFeatures.name: Top Destinations Chart
dashboardFeatures.description: Horizontal bar chart showing which countries receive most international students

dashboardFeatures.name: Global Trends Line Chart
dashboardFeatures.description: Time series showing total international student flows 2015-2023, with COVID period highlighted

dashboardFeatures.name: Pre/Post COVID Comparison
dashboardFeatures.description: Side-by-side comparison of 2019 vs. 2023 with recovery percentage

dashboardFeatures.name: Year-over-Year Growth Chart
dashboardFeatures.description: Percentage change in flows year-to-year, color-coded for growth/decline

dashboardFeatures.name: Detailed Flow Table
dashboardFeatures.description: Searchable table of top 50 bilateral corridors with flow counts and percentages

dashboardFeatures.name: Statistical Summary Cards
dashboardFeatures.description: Quick metrics: top source country, top destination, total students, active corridors

keyFindings: COVID-19 caused 30-40% decline in international student mobility in 2020-2021
Partial recovery by 2023 suggests in-person international education remains valued
USA, UK, Australia receive 50-70% of global international students
China and India are dominant source countries (300k-400k students annually pre-COVID)
Emerging diversification to non-traditional destinations (Canada, Singapore) post-COVID
Major bilateral corridors heavily concentrated: top 10 flows represent 40-50% of total
Regional mobility patterns vary significantly (strong South-North flows, weak intra-regional)
practicalApplications: Policy makers: Understand export/import of talent; inform education strategy and visa policy
University leaders: Benchmark against peer institutions; identify new recruitment markets
Education researchers: Analyze pandemic disruption and recovery patterns
Student advisors: Understand destination preferences and emerging opportunity markets
International educators: Track sector recovery; inform recruitment and retention strategies
Data analysts: Explore global education market dynamics
additionalVisualizations: Interactive flow arrow sizing for visual emphasis on major corridors
Color-coded year-over-year change (green for growth, red for decline)
COVID-19 impact shading on timeline charts
Regional clustering patterns in bilateral flows
Concentration metrics (top 10 flows as % of total)
interactiveElements: Real-time chart updates on slider drag
Hover tooltips on bar charts with exact values
Searchable flow table with live filtering
Responsive design for desktop, tablet, mobile
dataQualityNotes: Bilateral data may have collection gaps—some zero flows reflect data limitations, not true absence
Flow definition varies by country (tertiary enrollment, credit-granting programs, etc.)
Some countries report with 1-2 year lag; 2023 data may be projected
Mode of study not distinguished (residential, online, hybrid)
Use for trends/patterns more confidently than precision accounting
projectNotes: This dashboard demonstrates application of international comparative education analysis to global student mobility patterns. The COVID-19 focus reveals both disruption and underlying resilience in international higher education. Arrow-scaled flow visualization makes major mobility corridors immediately apparent while supporting drill-down analysis of bilateral relationships. Responsive design enables use on desktop for research/policy work and mobile for accessible exploration.
futureEnhancements: Bilateral pathway explorer with qualitative insights
Financial layer (tuition costs, scholarship availability, ROI)
Field of study patterns (STEM, business, humanities flows)
Separation of remote/online flows from residential
